what_it_does: This JSON file is an interpretive ledger entry for the claude-ds project's Icons.jsx canvas. It records a neutral analysis of the component's responsibilities, behavior, patterns, risks, open questions, themes, and intended contribution to the fused system.
observations: Contains project, path, model, and a detailed what_it_does summary of the Icons canvas.
Documents that ICON_CATEGORIES is hardcoded and maps glyph names to groups like People, Files, Communication, and Architecture.
Notes that icon bodies are read live from Object.keys(window.CV_ICONS.data).
Describes a generation panel that calls window.CV_AI.complete with a fixed line-style prompt requesting four {name, body} proposals.
Records defensive JSON parsing of model replies, including a [...] regex fallback and filtering/capping to 8 entries.
States that adopt() mutates window.CV_ICONS.data directly, de-dupes names with random suffixes, and notifies via addGenerated.
Notes renderIcon applies tone colors and injects SVG bodies with dangerouslySetInnerHTML.
Documents per-icon refine, duplicate-as-variant, and session-only delete affordances.
Mentions a header link to an external static icon explorer at ../assets/icons/index.html.
Flags the use of window.prompt() for variant descriptions and a search-to-generate fallback on empty results.
standouts: Direct in-place mutation of window.CV_ICONS.data makes generated icons immediately usable across the running system.
Per-glyph refine and duplicate-as-variant generation preserve the enforced line-style constraints.
Empty-search state turns a failed search term directly into a seeded generation prompt.
conventions: Follows a generate -> proposals -> adopt flow for adding icons.
Parses model JSON defensively with a raw-then-regex fallback.
Routes all generation through window.CV_AI.complete rather than a raw model client.
Aliases React hooks with an _ic suffix for the shared bundle.
Keeps glyph SVG bodies in an external store (window.CV_ICONS.data) instead of inline.
concerns: adopt/refine/duplicate/delete mutate window.CV_ICONS.data directly and rely on parent re-render callbacks, mixing global mutation with React state.
SVG bodies from the model are injected via dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization.
Uses the blocking window.prompt() for variant input, inconsistent with the inline RefinePop used for refinement.
notes: Interpretive digest of the Icons canvas: a searchable, categorized, tone-switchable icon browser backed by a live shared store and Vi-powered generation/refinement/branching.
questions: Is mutating window.CV_ICONS.data in place the intended persistence model, and does it survive reload?
How does CV_ICONS.data relate to the static ../assets/icons explorer linked in the header?
What component is RefinePop and how is it implemented elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file describes an icon library with AI generation, and the recorded behavior aligns with browsing CV_ICONS.data and generating/refining via CV_AI.
apparent_themes: Brand line-style consistency enforced through generation prompts.
Live shared asset store as the source of truth.
Search as a launch point for generation.
intention_signals: It appears aimed at making the icon set both browsable and elastic: keep every glyph in one consistent line style, let users search or generate the exact glyphs they need, and refine or branch any glyph while preserving style invariants.
novelty: Per-glyph refine and duplicate-as-variant generation, plus a search-becomes-generation fallback, all writing into a live icon store.
contribution: Contributes a documented, searchable, categorized icon canvas with line-style-constrained Vi generation, per-icon refinement/duplication/deletion, and a live shared icon store integration.
summary_for_embedding: An interpretive ledger entry for a React icon canvas that browses a categorized SVG glyph library from CV_ICONS.data, supports search and tone switching, and generates or refines line-style icons via CV_AI.
intention_for_embedding: To record a neutral analysis of a canvas meant for browsing, searching, generating, refining, and branching brand-consistent line-style icons into a live shared store.
